description: Off-by-one heap write in the nginx resolver allows a remote attacker who can control DNS responses to overwrite memory and potentially achieve remote code execution.
nginx 0.7.12 to 1.17.6 with certain error_page configurations allows HTTP request smuggling.
HTTP/2 Data Dribble flood (Netflix bulletin) in nginx allows a peer to request large amounts of data, consuming excessive CPU and memory for a denial of service.
HTTP/2 Resource Loop flood in nginx allows an attacker to create many request streams and continually shuffle their priority, causing excessive CPU usage.
HTTP/2 0-Length Headers Leak in nginx allows a peer to send a stream of headers with a 0-length name and value, causing excessive memory allocation.
Excessive memory consumption in the nginx HTTP/2 implementation can be triggered to cause a denial of service.
Excessive CPU usage in the nginx HTTP/2 implementation can be triggered to cause a denial of service.
The ngx_http_mp4_module in nginx may cause a worker process crash or memory disclosure when processing a specially crafted mp4 file.
Integer overflow in the range filter module of nginx 0.5.6 to 1.13.2 allows an attacker to obtain potentially sensitive cached information via a crafted Range header.
NULL pointer dereference in ngx_chain_to_iovec in nginx 1.9.13 and earlier allows a denial of service via a crafted request that triggers temporary file writing.
The resolver in nginx 0.6.18 to 1.9.9 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (worker process crash) via a crafted UDP DNS response.
Use-after-free in the resolver in nginx 0.6.18 to 1.9.9 allows remote attackers to cause a worker process crash or potentially execute code via a crafted DNS response.
The resolver in nginx 0.6.18 to 1.9.9 does not limit CNAME resolution, allowing remote attackers to cause excessive resource consumption.
SMTP proxy in nginx 1.5.6 to 1.7.3 allows STARTTLS command injection, enabling a man-in-the-middle attacker to insert commands into the encrypted session.
nginx 0.5.6 to 1.7.4 with shared SSL session cache or ticket key allows remote attackers to reuse SSL sessions via a crafted handshake.
Stack-based buffer overflow in nginx 1.3.9 to 1.4.0 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a chunked Transfer-Encoding request with a large chunk size.
ngx_http_proxy_module in nginx 1.1.4 to 1.4.0 with proxy_pass and proxy_cache may allow a buffer over-read and memory disclosure via a crafted upstream response.
nginx 0.1.0 to 1.1.18 allows remote attackers to obtain sensitive memory contents via crafted backend responses with proxy or fastcgi.
nginx on Windows allows remote attackers to bypass intended access restrictions via certain encoded characters in the URI.
Buffer underflow in ngx_http_parse_complex_uri in nginx 0.1.0 to 0.8.13 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted HTTP requests.
The ngx_http_mp4_module in nginx may cause a worker process crash or memory overwrite via a specially crafted audio or video file when the mp4 directive is used.
The ngx_http_mp4_module in nginx may cause a worker process crash and memory disclosure via a specially crafted mp4 file processed by the mp4 directive.
The ngx_http_mp4_module in nginx may over-read memory and crash a worker process when processing a specially crafted mp4 file with the mp4 directive enabled.
NULL pointer dereference in nginx 0.1.0 to 0.8.14 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service via a crafted request.
